codes.description: The manifest is not well-formed YAML.
The manifest top level is a sequence or scalar instead of a mapping.
The top-level pack: field is absent.
The pack: field is not one of the supported spec versions.
The pack: field is present but not a valid version string.
A field required by the current spec version is absent.
The author block is absent.
The description field is absent or empty.
The data: block (reads/writes/stores/phones_home) is absent.
The skills: block is absent or empty.
The version field is not a semver major.minor.patch.
The description exceeds the per-spec character limit.
The license field is not an SPDX identifier.
A service referenced in requires.services is not in services.yaml.
A capability referenced in requires_capabilities is not in capabilities.yaml.
A skill's category is not in skill-categories.yaml.
The visibility field is not 'open' or 'sealed'.
The tier field is not 'certified', 'verified', or 'community'.
The author.type field is not 'first-party' or 'community'.
The readiness field is not in the closed enum.
The pricing.model field is not in the closed enum.
A value in data.stores is not in the closed enum.
A pack declares writes but has no corresponding gate acknowledgement.
visibility sealed but required seal metadata is absent.
A service operation referenced is not in that contract's published operations.
The name contains disallowed characters or is reserved.
Two skills in the pack share a name.
A bundled contract name does not match the namespacing pattern (root or org-slug/-prefixed) (DD-157).
A sealed pack's signature does not validate against the author's published key.
A sealed pack's HMAC does not match the cleartext payload.
A tunable's type field is not in the closed type vocabulary.
Two tunables on the same skill share a key.
A tunable carries constraints incompatible with its declared type (e.g. min on a string, values on an int).
A tunable default, description, or skill prompt_delta contains a banned token (e.g. {{tool., <tool_use>, system_prompt:).
A tunable key does not match the snake_case pattern.

A pack declares an agent with a name reserved for the platform — the bundled stallari-core operator slugs (pkm-operator, comms-operator, scheduling-operator, secops-operator, fleet-operator, notifications-operator, home-operator, bizops-operator, review-operator, memory-operator) and the harness-only personas (digital-assistant, system-architect). Reference these via `skill.agent: <name>` to reuse the platform persona; redefining them is rejected. See docs/operators-v1.md.
The top-level spec_version: field is absent. Required at v3.0.0 (DD-172 P0). See docs/spec-version.md and docs/migration-v2-to-v3.md.
The pack declares track_workflows: which is retired in v3.0.0 (DD-260 Phase A). Migrate to the unified workflows: field with node-shaped graphs — see docs/migration-v2-to-v3.md.

The pack declares both workflows: and track_workflows:. The latter is retired in v3.0.0 (DD-260 Phase A); remove it. Pre-cut packs that mid-migrate carry both fields are explicitly rejected by the schema.

The skill graph_config declares classify_model: or escalation_model: which are hard-removed in v3.0.0 (DD-261 B.1). Use classify_tier: / escalation_tier: instead — DD-138/DD-236 capabilities supersede model pinning.

The plugin manifest declares install.network_transport which is hard-removed in v3.0.0 (DD-242 lean-pull, DD-261 audit-confirmed). The harness always spawns blade MCPs via stdio per DD-242 Phase A; the field had zero runtime consumers and is reintroduced when DD-242 Phase D marketplace gate ships with a real consumer.
